hero.title: The best choice for your pet
hero.lead: Alfavet in Baku brings examination, diagnostics, vaccination and paperwork into one clear appointment-led experience.

aboutPage.heroTitle: Veterinary care for pets in Baku
aboutPage.heroLead: Alfavet brings medical care, diagnostics and paperwork into one clinic, so owners do not need to visit several places for examination and treatment.

stats.label: address for examination, diagnostics and care

aboutPage.storyTitle: The goal is simple: medical precision with a feeling of trust
aboutPage.storyText: The clinic was created to provide professional medical help in an atmosphere of care and trust. The team treats patients and explains the situation clearly to owners.

storyBlocks.title: The goal is simple: medical precision with a feeling of trust
storyBlocks.description: The clinic was created to provide professional medical help in an atmosphere of care and trust. The team treats patients and explains the situation clearly to owners.

storyBlocks.title: Separate rooms for calmer examinations
storyBlocks.description: Pets are examined without contact with other animals. This helps reduce stress and gives the doctor space to speak calmly with the owner.

storyBlocks.title: Tests, X-ray and ultrasound in one clinic
storyBlocks.description: A laboratory, X-ray room and ultrasound equipment help the team make faster, more precise treatment decisions.

aboutPage.ctaText: Visits are handled by appointment so the team can give you and your pet focused, calm time.

items.question: Do I need to book an appointment in advance?
items.answer: Yes. Visits are handled by appointment so the team can give every patient calmer, more focused attention.

items.question: When can a puppy or kitten receive the first vaccine?
items.answer: The first vaccine is often planned from 6-8 weeks of age, but the vet makes the final call after an examination.

items.question: Can I get a veterinary passport at the clinic?
items.answer: Yes. Alfavet can prepare a veterinary passport and related documents.

items.question: Do you perform sterilization and castration?
items.answer: Yes. The vet examines the pet first and may request tests before surgery.

items.question: Which animals does the clinic accept?
items.answer: The clinic mainly accepts cats and dogs. For exotic pets or other small animals, it is best to confirm by phone first.
